headline: PE's New Playbook: Ops > Finance in 2026

stories.title: Physician Groups Level Up Beyond Buy-and-Build
stories.summary: Healthcare PE is ditching raw consolidation for AI-powered ops. Cardinal Health's GI Alliance acquisition strategy shows how distributors are winning by embedding efficiency tech, revenue cycle management, and physician alignment instead of just stacking practices.
stories.takeaway: Operational excellence and AI deployment are now table stakes—groups pushing performance analytics and care coordination models will dominate exits.

stories.title: Retail's Margin Play: Private Label + AI Personalization
stories.summary: Walgreens-style take-privates are using supply chain efficiency and store-brand expansion to build defensible margins. PE buyers targeting retail are now focused on operational discipline plus omnichannel personalization—not just closing stores.
stories.takeaway: Margin expansion via private label, RMN monetization, and AI-driven pricing will unlock 2–4% gross margin gains faster than legacy playbooks.

stories.title: AI Data Centers + Energy Reshoring Drive Manufacturing M&A
stories.summary: Industrial PE is chasing supply chain resilience and AI infrastructure demand. Chart Industries–Flowserve merger ($19B) exemplifies how scale in thermal management and energy systems commands premiums as reshoring and decarbonization tailwinds accelerate.
stories.takeaway: Manufacturing buyers winning on automation, predictive maintenance, and EV/clean energy exposure—tariff-proof supply chains are now a valuation lever.

stories.title: Healthcare IT: Rule of 60 via Pricing + AI Automation
stories.summary: Bain Capital's HealthEdge–HealthProof merger shows tech PE shifting from user-adoption metrics to Rule of 60 economics. Gen AI in RCM stacks, pricing optimization, and cross-sell are now baseline value levers—platforms winning on measurable ROI to end customers.
stories.takeaway: Healthcare IT deals outperforming broader PE because AI acts as a revenue accelerator, not just cost-cutting tool. Defensible platforms with embedded compliance data hold premium exit multiples.

watchNext.title: McKinsey Global Private Markets Report 2026: Mature PE Demands Operational Rigor
watchNext.why: Deep dive on the 'new math for PE'—LPs now rank value creation strategy as top-3 manager selection criteria, pushing GPs to invest in in-house operating teams and AI playbooks rather than leverage arbitrage.

watchNext.title: Bain 2026 Global PE Report: '12 is the New 5' – Extended Hold Periods Demand Different Value Levers
watchNext.why: With median hold periods hitting 6.7 years, GPs need sustainable EBITDA growth, not multiple arbitrage. Report shows secondary markets hitting $240B in 2025 and continuation vehicles at $115B—reshaping exit strategies.
